LAW AND MOTION 302, DEC-06-2024 HEARING ON MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-02-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT, FILED DECEMBER 9, 2024, WHICH IS UNOPPOSED, IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO APPROVE SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(F) AND CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6 IS GRANTED AND JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED CONSISTENT WITH THE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND THE [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED. CONSENT JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (301/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT. MATTER CONTESTED. ARGUMENT HELD. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE CASE IS STAYED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE ATTORNEY FEES. A STATUS CONFERENCE IS SET FOR NOVEMBER 8, 2024, AT 9:30 A.M. ON THE LAW & MOTION CALENDAR. A JOINT CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS DUE BY NOVEMBER 1, 2024. THE COURT TO CHECK WITH OTHER JUDGES TO ARRANGE A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE BETWEEN THE PARTIES. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR THE COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. NOT REPORTED. BAILIFF: NOT PRESENT. (301/RCE)

Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-09-2024 Amended

MOTION / Amended Notice Of Motion & Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-02-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Jay Epps's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment, filed December 9, 2024, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted and judgment shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Order signed. Consent Judgment signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Clifford A. Chanler, Esq., of Chanler, LLC, (475) 277-2932, for Plaintiff.

Gregory S. Berlin, Esq., of Alston & Bird LLP, (213) 576-1000 for Defendant.

Susan S. Fiering, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, (510) 622-2270, for Office of the California Attorney General.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is denied without prejudice. In light of the proposed amendments to the settlement's terms, Plaintiff may file a new motion that allows the court to make its required findings per Health & Safety Code 25249.7(f)(4)-(5).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2024 Continued To Jan-15-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Continued To Dec-06-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Clifford A. Chanler, Esq. of Chanler LLC, Tel. 415.277.2932, appearing for Plaintiff Jay Epps;
Gregory S. Berlin, Esq. of Alston & Bird LLP, Tel. 213.576.1000, appearing for Defendant Walmart, Inc.;
Susan S. Fiering, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.879.1300, appearing for the Office of the Attorney General.

A Case Management Conference is held regarding the issue of the attorney's fee.

The Court noted that there was a settlement conference with Judge Suzanne Bolanos.

Mr. Chanler provided a brief update stating that subsequent to the settlement conference with Judge Bolanos that he and Mr. Berlin held discussions on an alternative method re: settlement. Mr. Chanler also stated that revisions were sent to Mr. Berlin (Walmart), that Walmart has not had time to respond, that there are some modifications as to the fee issue, and that the parties will continue to work today on the fee issue which the court left for the parties to negotiate.

The matter is continued to December 6, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. on the Law & Motion calendar for the Court to receive and review the new consent decree.

A copy of the new consent decree is to be submitted with a courtesy copy to Dept. 302 by close of business (4:00 p.m.) on Wednesday, November 27, 2024.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Bailiff: Park. (301/RCE)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Continued To Nov-13-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-20-2024 Continued To Nov-08-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Clifford A. Chanler, Esq.
Gregory S. Berlin, Esq.
Susan Fiering, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. for the California Office of the Attorney General.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

This matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications:

Plaintiff Jay Epps's motion for approval of Proposition 65 Settlement and for entry of consent judgment is denied.

The following modifications are ordered:

The case is stayed with the exception of the attorney fees.

A status conference is set for November 8, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. on the Law & Motion calendar.

A joint case management conference is due by November 1, 2024.

The Court is to check with other judges for a settlement conference between the parties.

The Court notes that the AG is allowed to file a sur-reply.

(See Word minutes for the complete ruling.)

Court to review the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Jamie Hong, CSR #11975, Tel: Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)
